---
title: "Linear Models"
---

## Problem setup

Given feature vectors $\mathbf{x}$ and targets $y$, fit parameters $(\mathbf{w}, b)$ to minimize prediction error.

## Objective

For regression, the mean squared error objective is:

$$
L(\mathbf{w}, b) = \frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N}(\hat{y}_i - y_i)^2
$$

with $\hat{y}_i = \mathbf{w}^T\mathbf{x}_i + b$.

## Why it matters

Linear models are simple enough to reason about and form the conceptual bridge to neural networks.
For optimization intuition, revisit @fig-training-surface from the foundations chapter.
